summary: Mixed recording quality with muffled audio, but a solid spring‑92 show highlighted by So Many Roads, Black Peter and a strong SMR debut.
review: Fans recall a warm, laid‑back Atlanta night that opened the 1992 spring tour. The performance is praised for standout moments like So Many Roads, Black Peter, Stuck Inside of Mobile Homes and a rocking Picasso Moon, while keyboard work from Vince (and Bruce’s presence) receives kudos. Recording-wise opinions diverge – some describe it as a little muffled or even terrible, indicating only fair sound quality.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is generally positive (good song choices and band energy) but recording issues are noted by several reviewers; the combined weighted estimate lands in the low‑mid‑3 range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Several listeners call the audio muffled or terrible, though one notes it becomes acceptable after adjusting. No clear indication of soundboard vs audience source.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A solid, laid‑back opening to the spring tour with a well‑received second set; fans note good momentum despite modest excitement levels.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His vocal delivery on So Many Roads is praised and overall presence is celebrated.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work described as shaking the upper sections like ocean waves – very positive.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s vocals/keys get attention; Bruce’s continued presence noted positively.
